name: Pasta with Mixed Roasted Peppers Vinaigrette
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Balsamic or good-quality red wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast, peel, stem, and seed the peppers.
To roast peppers: Place them in a foil-lined baking sheet in an oven preheated to 500 degrees Fahrenheit. Roast until their skins are evenly blistered and browned, about 25 minutes, turning them 2 or 3 times so they roast evenly. Remove from the oven and cover with a kitchen towel.
- When peppers are cool enough to handle, pull out their stems, peel away their blackened skins; open the peppers up; and remove their seeds using a teaspoon to pick up any stray ones.
- Tear the peppers into 1/2-inch wide strips and save their juices. Set aside.
2. In a large skillet, heat olive oil with garlic over moderate heat until it sizzles.
3. Add the pepper strips and their juices to the skillet.
4. Stir in the vinegar. As soon as the liquid sizzles, stir in the basil and parsley.
5. Spoon the sauce over cooked pasta.
